segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: So, Tim, we have to do a short summary of our work experience on a farm.
segments.zh: 蒂姆，我们得写一份关于农场工作经历的简短总结。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错。我去的农场很棒，但安排工作经历可不容易。一个问题是农场离得很远，我又不开车。而且，我本来想实习一个月，但只拿到了两周的。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: My farm was great, but arranging the work experience was hard.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: One problem was it was miles away and I don't drive.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: And also, I'd really wanted a placement for a month, but I could only get one for two weeks.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: I was lucky, the farmer let me stay so I didn't have to travel.
segments.zh: 我很幸运，农场主让我住下，所以不用来回跑。但找到合适的农场申请并不容易。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: But finding the right sort of farm to apply to wasn't easy.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: No, they don't seem to have websites, do they.
segments.zh: 是啊，他们好像没有网站，对吧。我是通过我妈的一个朋友找到的，但也不容易。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I found mine through a friend of my mother's, but it wasn't easy.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: No.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: My farm was mostly livestock, especially sheep.
segments.zh: 我的农场主要是牲畜，尤其是羊。我很喜欢帮忙照料它们。有一晚我几乎整夜没睡，帮一只母羊接生小羊羔……

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I really enjoyed helping out with them.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I was up most of one night helping a sheep deliver a lamb …

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: On your own?
segments.zh: 你一个人？

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: No, the farmer was there, and he told me what to do.
segments.zh: 不是，农场主也在，他告诉我怎么做。那次分娩不太顺利，但我搞定了。看到小羊羔摇摇晃晃站起来，几乎立刻就开始吃奶，知道它没事了，那种感觉真好。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: It wasn't a straightforward birth, but I managed.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: It was a great feeling to see the lamb stagger to its feet and start feeding almost straightaway, and to know that it was OK.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Mm.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Then another time a lamb had broken its leg, and they got the vet in to set it, and he talked me through what he was doing.
segments.zh: 还有一次，一只羊羔摔断了腿，他们请兽医来给它接骨，兽医一边做一边给我讲解。那真的很有用。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: That was really useful.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Yes, my farm had sheep too.
segments.zh: 是啊，我那个农场也养羊。农场在一个山谷里，养的是低地品种，叫萨福克羊，不过农场主说他们以前也养过其他品种。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: The farm was in a valley and they had a lowland breed called Suffolks, although the farmer said they'd had other breeds in the past.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: So were they bred for their meat?
segments.zh: 那它们是为了产肉而饲养的吗？

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Mostly, yes.
segments.zh: 主要是的。它们个头挺大，很结实。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: They're quite big and solid.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: My farm was up in the hills and they had a different breed of sheep, they were Cheviots.
segments.zh: 我的农场在山上，养的是另一种羊，叫切维厄特羊。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Oh, I heard their wool's really sought after.
segments.zh: 哦，我听说它们的羊毛很受欢迎。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。羊毛很耐磨，用来做地毯。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: It's very hardwearing and they use it for carpets.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I was interested in the amount of supplements they add to animals' feed nowadays.
segments.zh: 我对现在给动物饲料里加那么多添加剂很感兴趣。比如，连鸡的饲料里都加了额外的维生素和电解质。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Like, even the chickens got extra vitamins and electrolytes in their feed.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Yes, I found that too.
segments.zh: 是啊，我也发现了。而且它们还不便宜。但我的农场主说有些东西定价过高。他并不是常规性地给它们，只是在鸡似乎特别需要的时候才给。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: And they're not cheap.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: But my farmer said some are overpriced for what they are.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: And he didn't give them as a matter of routine, just at times when the chickens seemed to particularly require them.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Yes, mine said the same.
segments.zh: 是的，我的农场主也这么说。他说某些品种的鸡可能比其他品种需要更多补充剂，但便宜的和贵的其实都差不多。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: He said certain breeds of chickens might need more supplements than the others, but the cheap and expensive ones are all basically the same.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Mm.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: So did your farm have any other livestock, Diana?
segments.zh: 戴安娜，你的农场还有其他牲畜吗？

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Yes, dairy cows.
segments.zh: 有，奶牛。我在挤奶棚里犯了一个很尴尬的错误。有些牛用了抗生素，所以它们的奶不适合人类饮用，必须放在单独的容器里。但我搞混了，把一头不对的牛的奶倒进了给人喝的奶里，结果整批奶都得扔掉。农场主对我很不高兴。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: I made a really embarrassing mistake when I was working in the milk shed.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Some cows had been treated with antibiotics, so their milk wasn't suitable for human consumption, and it had to be put in a separate container.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: But I got mixed up, and I poured some milk from the wrong cow in with the milk for humans, so the whole lot had to be thrown away.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: The farmer wasn't too happy with me.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I asked my farmer how much he depended on the vet to deal with health problems.
segments.zh: 我问我的农场主他在多大程度上依赖兽医处理健康问题。我读过一些报道，说由于农民面临增产压力，牲畜的健康正受到影响。嗯，他不同意这一点，但他说实际上兽医做的有些事情，比如小手术，他自己完全有能力做。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I'd read reports that the livestock's health is being affected as farmers are under pressure to increase production.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Well, he didn't agree with that, but he said that actually some of the stuff the vets do, like minor operations, he'd be quite capable of doing himself.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊，我的农场主也这么说。但他觉得兽医的技能还是需要的。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: My farmer said the same.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: But he reckons vets' skills are still needed.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Now we've got to give a bit of feedback about last term's modules – just short comments, apparently.
segments.zh: 现在我们需要对上学期的模块给点反馈——只是简短评论，显然。我们现在做这个吗？

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Shall we do that now?

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的，那么医学术语。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: So medical terminology.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Well, my heart sank when I saw that, especially right at the beginning of the course.
segments.zh: 嗯，我看到那个的时候心就沉了，尤其是在课程刚开始的时候。而且我确实学得很吃力。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: And I did struggle with it.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I'd thought it'd be hard, but actually I found it all quite straightforward.
segments.zh: 我原以为会很难，但实际上我觉得挺简单的。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: What did you think about diet and nutrition?
segments.zh: 你觉得饮食与营养那个模块怎么样？

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Do you remember what they told us about pet food and the fact that there's such limited checking into whether or not it's contaminated?
segments.zh: 你还记得他们跟我们说的关于宠物食品的事吗？实际上对它们是否受污染的检查非常有限。我是说，跟人类食品的检查相比——我觉得那太糟糕了。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I mean in comparison with the checks on food for humans – I thought that was terrible.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Mm.
segments.zh: 嗯，我觉得给我印象最深的是动物疾病那个模块，我们研究了世界不同地区的家养动物，比如骆驼、水牛和羊驼。这么多国家的经济都依赖这些动物，但科学家对影响它们的疾病了解不多。

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: I think the module that really impressed me was the animal disease one, when we looked at domesticated animals in different parts of the world, like camels and water buffalo and alpaca.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: The economies of so many countries depend on these, but scientists don't know much about the diseases that affect them.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Yes, I thought they'd know a lot about ways of controlling and eradicating those diseases, but that's not the case at all.
segments.zh: 是啊，我原以为他们对控制和根除这些疾病的方法很了解，但根本不是那么回事。我很喜欢野生动物医疗这个单元，比如帮助那些被石油泄漏困住的鸟类，这是我以前没想过的。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: I loved the wildlife medication unit.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Things like helping birds that have been caught in oil spills.

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: That's something I hadn't thought about before.

segments.speaker: DIANA
segments.en: Yeah, I thought I might write my dissertation on something connected with that.
segments.zh: 是啊，我想我的论文可能会写和这个相关的内容。

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的，那么……

segments.speaker: TIM
segments.en: So ...

items.prompt: What problem did both Diana and Tim have when arranging their work experience?

pairs.note: 题干和原文都用了problem，直接对应。
题干和原文几乎一样，只是their换成了the。

pairs.p: My farm was great, but arranging the work experience was hard.
pairs.note: 原文中Diana用第一人称描述自己的经历，题干问两人共同的问题，需从上下文判断。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句是Diana描述自己安排工作经历时遇到的困难，题干问两人共同的问题，因此需结合上下文找到Tim的类似表述。

items.prompt: Tim was pleased to be able to help

pairs.q: pleased to be able to help
pairs.note: pleased to be able to help 与 enjoyed helping out 都表示乐于帮忙，是近义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干说Tim乐于帮忙，定位句提到他enjoyed helping out，通过同义替换找到答案。

items.prompt: Diana says the sheep on her farm

pairs.note: 原文用'they'指代题干中的'sheep on her farm'，是代词指代。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Diana说她农场的羊如何，定位句以'So were they bred for their meat?'开头，'they'即指代农场的羊，通过代词指代定位。

items.prompt: What did the students learn about adding supplements to chicken feed?

pairs.note: learn about 替换为 found，表示发现/了解到。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的关键词 'adding supplements to chicken feed' 在原文中直接定位到相同短语，然后根据 'learn about' 对应 'found' 找到后续信息。

items.prompt: What happened when Diana was working with dairy cows?

pairs.note: 题干说'与奶牛一起工作'，原文说'在挤奶棚工作'，意思相同但表达不同。

pairs.p: I made a really embarrassing mistake
pairs.note: 题干问'发生了什么'，原文直接说'犯了一个尴尬的错误'，这是对事件的直接描述。
题干和原文都出现了'dairy cows'，这是关键词原词重现。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'dairy cows'和'working'定位到原文第一句，然后听到'embarrassing mistake'就知道事件开始了。

items.prompt: What did both farmers mention about vets and farming?

pairs.p: he said that actually some of the stuff the vets do
pairs.note: 题干说'both farmers mention'，但原文只提到一个农民，所以这里用'he said'对应，注意是间接提及
题干中的'vets'直接对应原文中的'the vets'，'farming'隐含在上下文中

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到农民说兽医做的某些事（如小手术）他自己也能做，这与题干问的'农民提到兽医和农业的关系'相关，但注意原文只提到一个农民，题干说'both'可能是个陷阱。

items.prompt: What opinion do the students give about each of the following modules on their veterinary science course?

pairs.note: 题干中的hard与原文中的hard完全一致，表示‘困难’
题干中的straightforward与原文中的straightforward完全一致，表示‘简单易懂’

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的hard和straightforward直接对应原文中的相同词汇，定位到学生认为该模块并不困难，而是简单易懂，从而得出答案为A。

items.prompt: What opinion do the students give about each of the following modules on their veterinary science course?

pairs.note: opinion 对应 thought that was terrible，表达负面看法。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问学生对模块的看法，定位句中学生说'thought that was terrible'，直接对应opinion，且terrible是负面评价，与答案E（表示负面）一致。

items.prompt: What opinion do the students give about each of the following modules on their veterinary science course?

pairs.note: 题干问opinion，原文'I think'直接引出观点，是定位词。
题干问opinion，原文'impressed me'表示正面评价，对应题干。
题干模块名，原文直接出现，帮助定位。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'opinion'和'impressed'，在原文中找到'I think'和'impressed me'，并匹配模块名'animal disease'，从而定位到答案F。

items.prompt: What opinion do the students give about each of the following modules on their veterinary science course?

pairs.note: opinion 对应 thought，表示观点
students 对应 I，这里学生用第一人称表达
give about 被具体化为 write my dissertation on，表示对模块的看法

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位句中的 'I thought I might write my dissertation on something connected with that' 可以判断学生对该模块持积极态度，因此选择 C。
